stories.headline: Supreme Court blocks Trump's attempt to fire Federal Reserve governor Lisa Cook
stories.summary: The decision, seen as a win for central bank independence, sends the fight over removal back to the lower courts.

stories.headline: Supreme Court blocks Trump from sacking Fed governor Cook
stories.summary: Justices say president can fire top regulator in separate decision

stories.headline: US says it has agreed deal with Iran to halt strikes and resume talks
stories.summary: White House announces Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will fly to Doha for ‘high-level meetings’ but Tehran disputes that further negotiations have been confirmed

stories.headline: Trump's final appeal of E Jean Carroll sex abuse case rejected
stories.summary: The Supreme Court decision means the US president will now have to pay Carroll the $5m (£3.6m) in damages she had been awarded.

stories.headline: Supreme Court protects the Federal Reserve, but what about everybody else?
stories.summary: While the central bank’s independence was upheld, other agencies were not so lucky

stories.headline: Supreme Court Expands Trump’s Power to Fire Officials, but Blocks Fed Governor Dismissal
stories.summary: In twin rulings, the justices said President Trump could fire independent regulators for any reason. But they affirmed the Fed’s independence, and said its leaders could not be fired at will.

stories.headline: Supreme Court Rejects Trump’s Request to Appeal $5 Million Verdict in E. Jean Carroll Case
stories.summary: President Trump had asked the justices to intervene after a jury found that he had sexually abused and defamed the writer E. Jean Carroll.

stories.headline: Cook Stays at Fed But Trump Wins Power Over Other Agencies
stories.summary: A closely divided US Supreme Court reinforced the Federal Reserve’s independence from the White House, protecting governors from being fired by the president without proof of wrongdoing.

stories.headline: Supreme Court, for now, blocks Trump from firing Fed board member Lisa Cook
stories.summary: The decision is a setback for Trump, who alleged that Cook committed mortgage fraud. She is the first Black woman to serve on the Fed board.

stories.headline: Supreme Court lets stand $5 million civil verdict against Trump in Carroll case
stories.summary: A 2023 trial found President Donald Trump liable for allegedly sexually assaulting journalist E. Jean Carroll. The decision Monday leaves in place a ruling affirming the judgment.

stories.headline: Trump Renews Threat to Fire Fed Governor in Wake of Court Loss
stories.summary: The president promised to “take appropriate action immediately” against Lisa D. Cook, a Fed governor.

stories.headline: Supreme Court blocks Trump’s bid to fire US Federal Reserve’s Lisa Cook
stories.summary: Supreme Court blocks Trump's attempt to fire Fed governor Lisa Cook over unproven mortgage fraud allegations

stories.headline: US Supreme Court rebuffs Trump’s appeal in E Jean Carroll case
stories.summary: The US Supreme Court has rejected a push by President Trump to throw out a jury's findings of sexual assault.

stories.headline: Russian missile and drone attacks kill at least eight in Ukraine
stories.summary: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy condemns the 'horrific attacks' and urges allies to boost Ukraine's air defences.

stories.headline: US says it has agreed to 'stand down' after exchange of strikes with Iran
stories.summary: A series of strikes over the weekend saw the US and Iran accuse each other of violating the ceasefire agreement.

stories.headline: Supreme Court Rules Against Roy Moore in Fight Over $8.2 Million Jury Award
stories.summary: The former Alabama Supreme Court justice and Senate candidate had asked the court to clear a path for him to potentially collect a jury award in a defamation case.

stories.headline: Five people killed in shooting in northern Germany, police say – Europe live
stories.summary: German police say two people were apprehended after ‘numerous’ shots were fired at a youth care facility in StadeMeanwhile, Ukraine is bracing to absorb the impact of the heatwave on its energy network, already pummelled by Russian attacks over more than four years of war, AFP reported.Grid operators in at least five regions – from Ivano-Frankivsk in the west to Zaporizhzhia on the frontline in the south – announced temporary restrictions on ener

stories.headline: Afghan families mourn loved ones as border tensions with Pakistan rise
stories.summary: Afghan officials say Pakistani attacks killed at least 36 civilians, while Pakistan says its forces killed 29 fighters.

stories.headline: Sudan’s el-Obeid: Life on the edge of collapse
stories.summary: Sudan's el-Obeid faces mass atrocities, like el-Fasher in 2025, the UN warns.

stories.headline: DR Congo bans mass gatherings in the  capital to prevent spread of Ebola
stories.summary: Opposition politicians accuse the government of using the outbreak to halt a planned protest.

stories.headline: Teyana Taylor's tears and Lauryn Hill tribute steal show at BET Awards
stories.summary: Taylor had an emotional reaction to being presented with the Icon of the Year award by Janet Jackson.

stories.headline: Senior South African police officer survives assassination attempt
stories.summary: Deputy crime intelligence boss Maj-Gen Feroz Khan was due to appear before an inquiry into police corruption this week.

stories.headline: HDFC Bank Appoints Ex-Bureaucrat Rajiv Kumar as Part-Time Chair
stories.summary: HDFC Bank Ltd. named former finance secretary, Rajiv Kumar, as its part-time chairman, months after the abrupt resignation of Atanu Chakraborty sparked questions about governance at the nation’s largest private lender.

stories.headline: June Payrolls Expected to Boost Rate-Hike Bets
stories.summary:
The FOMC’s tone at the June meeting, together with Chairman Kevin Warsh’s news conference, has raised the prospect of a near-term rate hike. 
Aditya Bhave, Head: US Economics at Bank of America, discusses the Fed and his interest rate outlook for the remainder of 2026. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Shares in chipmakers underpinning AI boom rocket in first half of 2026
stories.summary: Value of some chip manufacturers have tripled, or more, driving Asia Pacific stock markets sharply higherShares in chipmakers have surged in the first half of this year as investors piled into companies that make the hardware underpinning the AI boom, according to analysis.Investors have driven up the value of semiconductor and memory chip manufacturers, whose profits have soared during 2026, at the expense of some large software companies, which

stories.headline: Europe heatwave shows need to reject climate denial ‘lies’, says EU green chief
stories.summary: Teresa Ribera blames ‘ideologically driven’ falsehoods, driven by those with vested interests in fossil fuels, for attacks on green policyThe heatwave wreaking chaos across Europe is a “dramatic warning” to reject climate naysayers, a European Commission vice-president says.Teresa Ribera, executive vice-president for a clean, just and competitive transition, lambasted those who listened to the “vested interests” of the fossil fuel industry rather

stories.headline: Why Pakistan’s Afghan air strikes aren’t stopping armed attacks
stories.summary: Analysts warn military pressure alone cannot contain a threat from armed groups that has reached Pakistan's cities.

stories.headline: Pakistani strikes kill at least 28 civilians in Afghanistan, UN says
stories.summary: Pakistan claims it was targeting militants near its border, but the Taliban government calls it an "atrocity".

stories.headline: South Korea football coach quits as president calls for probe into World Cup loss
stories.summary: South Korea was eliminated after missing out on a spot among the eight best third-placed teams.

stories.headline: South Korea unveils $880bn chip and AI investment plan
stories.summary: It comes as regional rivals like Taiwan, China and Japan are investing heavily in chip factories and other technology.

stories.headline: China grounds light aircraft after Beijing tower crash
stories.summary: Flight operators say authorities issued indefinite nationwide airspace controls

stories.headline: Charter Junk Bonds, CDS See Record Moves on Bets of Comcast Deal
stories.summary: Some Charter Communications Inc. debt securities saw record improvement Monday, as traders bet that rival Comcast Corp.’s pending breakup could result in a combination of the two broadband heavyweights.

stories.headline: Owners Keep Sending Oil Tankers Through Hormuz After Attacks
stories.summary: Oil tankers continued navigating the Strait of Hormuz, despite a pair of attacks over the past several days that made some owners more cautious about navigating the world’s top maritime chokepoint.

stories.headline: Yen Hits 40-Year Low in Historic Slide That’s Rattled Japan
stories.summary: The yen slid to its weakest level against the dollar since 1986, a milestone that will generate unease in Japan and put traders on high alert for authorities wading into the market.

stories.headline: Del Monte Corporation Reunites After 37 Years
stories.summary: Del Monte Corporation Chairman and CEO Mohammad Abu-Ghazaleh joined Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why the iconic food brand reunited after nearly four decades and discusses easing supply chain pressures. He also reveals some surprising pharmaceutical research using pineapple-derived compounds (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Verizon, BT Merge International Units in Home Markets Pivot
stories.summary: Verizon Communications Inc. and the UK’s BT Group Plc agreed to create a joint venture for their international businesses in a merger that will take the low-margin units off their books and allow the carriers to focus on their home markets.

stories.headline: See How the Makeup of Independent Agencies Has Changed Under Trump
stories.summary: Even before the Supreme Court granted him more power to fire officials, President Trump had effectively ended Democratic majorities at several agencies.

stories.headline: Venezuela earthquake: father and son found alive in rubble after four days as death toll nears 1,500
stories.summary: President says ‘we always hold onto hope’ as discovery of earthquake survivors spurs fresh search efforts despite dwindling chances of survivalMortuary in Caracas ‘overwhelmed’ as Venezuela struggles to respond after earthquakesA man and his teenage son were found alive under the rubble in Venezuela on Sunday, in a town about 40km north of the capital Caracas, AFP journalists reported, as the death toll from last week’s twin earthquakes passed 1,

So I’ll be watching on the TV. And like I’ve said before, sometimes I’m the minister for sport and it’s important I’m at major events because there’s

stories.headline: Fire and floods hit Ghana’s capital as residents are left stranded
stories.summary: A massive fire engulfed a rubber factory in Ghana’s capital, Accra, as severe flooding swamped parts of the city.

stories.headline: Earthquake jolts southwest China
stories.summary: An earthquake with a magnitude of 5.5 struck Yibin in China’s southwest province of Sichuan shortly on Monday.

Pakistan airstrikes leave homes in ruins in Afghanistan
stories.summary: Villagers inspect destroyed homes in Afghanistan's Paktia province after overnight airstrikes, as Afghan and Pakistani officials give differing accounts.

stories.headline: US and Iran exchange strikes and accuse each other of violating ceasefire
stories.summary: Iran says it has launched retaliatory attacks at US infrastructure in Kuwait and Bahrain after the US said it hit multiple targets across Iran.

stories.headline: Europe's heatwave linked to 1,300 deaths, WHO says, as Germany hits record 41.7C
stories.summary: WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus warned that Europe is not prepared for high temperatures.

stories.headline: Three firefighters die battling huge wildfires on Colorado-Utah border
stories.summary: A number of blazes have been raging across Utah and Colorado, leading both governors to declare states of emergency.

stories.headline: Fourteen killed in Saudi Arabia helicopter crash
stories.summary: The aircraft belonged to state oil giant Aramco and the cause of the crash is being investigated.

stories.headline: Oil Rises as US and Iran Halt Attacks, But Hormuz Traffic Slows
stories.summary: Oil rose as traders assessed upcoming peace talks between the US and Iran following a weekend of flare-ups over the Strait of Hormuz, including an attack on a supertanker laden with crude.

stories.headline: Big oil’s secretive trading arms are having an extraordinary year
stories.summary: Meet the corporate gamblers who never waste a good energy crisis

stories.headline: Tech Giants Power Stock Gains After AI-Fueled Rout: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: A rally in several technology giants lifted stocks as the recent artificial-intelligence selloff lured buyers betting the industry’s investment boom will continue to support solid corporate earnings.

stories.headline: New Caledonia polls open in first local vote in the French territory since 2019
stories.summary: Election will determine balance of power in New Caledonia before fresh negotiations with France on the territory’s statusPolls opened in the French overseas territory of New Caledonia on Sunday for the archipelago’s first provincial elections since 2019, after the vote was delayed as talks stalled over its political future.The election, initially planned for 2024, will determine the balance of power in New Caledonia ahead of fresh negotiations wi

stories.headline: Israel strikes southern Lebanon as Hezbollah condemns new deal
stories.summary: Strikes have killed at least one person, state media say a day after Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement.

stories.headline: Families calling out to loved ones trapped in rubble by Venezuela quakes
stories.summary: In La Guaira, desperate families keep vigil at buildings where they fear their relatives are trapped, but face an impossible task to move heavy debris.

stories.headline: US strikes Iran after attack on cargo ship
stories.summary: Iran accuses the US of violating their deal and says it struck targets linked to American forces in response.

stories.headline: Why Kim Jong Un never talks about his mother - or her controversial bloodline
stories.summary: Very few North Koreans know about her, as her origin could threaten the regime’s legitimacy.

stories.headline: Watch: A race against the clock for Venezuela earthquake rescuers
stories.summary: There is a sense that the next few hours will be critical for saving anyone who is still trapped, says the BBC's Dan Johnson reporting from the border of Venezuela and Columbia.

stories.headline: GOP candidate with same name as senator can stay on Alaska ballot, judge rules
stories.summary: Supporters of incumbent Republican Sen. Dan Sullivan say a challenger with the same name is not running in “good faith,” but a judge says that is not an issue.

stories.headline: Christmas market attacker jailed for life for murdering six in Germany
stories.summary: A nine-year-old and five women were killed when Taleb Al-Abdulmohsen drove into the market in 2024.

stories.headline: Israel and Lebanon sign framework agreement after US-brokered talks
stories.summary: Hezbollah is not party to Friday's agreement, and previous ceasefires between Israel and Lebanon have still seen near-daily cross-border strikes.

stories.headline: Trump accuses Iran of ceasefire breach after Strait of Hormuz attack
stories.summary: A Singapore-flagged ship was struck crossing the waterway on Thursday, prompting a large-scale evacuation to be paused.

stories.headline: Rainbow flags await Egypt and Iran at awkward Pride Match
stories.summary: Iran's coach refuses to discuss pride celebrations in Seattle, where his country face Egypt in a crucial Group G match.

stories.headline: Would farmers and fishermen back Brexit today?
stories.summary: Polls suggested the majority of farmers and fishers in England voted to leave the EU 10 years ago.

stories.headline: Outrage as woman jailed for three years after criticising Somali government online
stories.summary: Sentencing of 27-year-old Sadia Moalim Ali condemned by former president and prime ministers as well as rights groupsA rickshaw driver in Somalia has been sentenced to three years in prison for comments she made on social media, in a case that has caught the public’s attention and provoked outrage in the country.Sadia Moalim Ali, a 27-year-old nursing graduate, was originally charged with insulting government institutions and incitement to commit

stories.headline: Poland's Tusk calls for 'mutual respect' during row with Ukraine
stories.summary: Ukraine's Volodmyr Zelensky skips major conference aimed at rebuilding his country after being stripped of a Polish state honour.

stories.headline: ‘Constitutional coup’ claims as Zimbabwe senate approves extending presidential term

stories.headline: UPS is losing ground to FedEx
stories.summary: The two logistics giants have similar strategies—and face a common threat

stories.headline: UN nuclear chief says inspectors will visit Iran sites as part of war deal
stories.summary: Rafael Grossi says the IAEA is "working on modalities" but an Iranian  minister says such access would only be part of a final deal with the US.

stories.headline: Biggest city in Crimea without power after Ukraine strikes
stories.summary: Sevastopol's Moscow-installed governor warns there will be no electricity in some areas until the evening.

stories.headline: Strange new EV-makers keep appearing in China
stories.summary: If you can make a robo-vacuum, you can make a car

stories.headline: Silicon Valley has much to learn from the spreadsheet jockeys it despises
stories.summary: Even if financial markets disagree

stories.headline: Dozens of ships head through Strait of Hormuz after US-Iran deal
stories.summary: Traffic in the waterway has risen since the US and Iran signed a deal aimed at ending the war, including 42 ships on Saturday alone.

stories.headline: Israeli troops kill two in south Lebanon after lull in fighting, authorities say
stories.summary: Israel's military says the two men were Hezbollah operatives, but the Iran-backed group accuses it of a ceasefire violation.

stories.headline: UN commission of inquiry says Israel committing genocide in Gaza by deliberately targeting children
stories.summary: Israel rejects the new report by the three-member expert panel, calling it a "libellous sham".

stories.headline: Leading Pakistan activist given life sentence over soldier's killing at rally
stories.summary: Human rights campaigner Mahrang Baloch denied inciting protesters before the official's death.

stories.headline: 'I witnessed Maradona's Hand of God' - a goal still talked about 40 years on
stories.summary: One BBC journalist's first football game had one of the sport's most controversial moments and also one of the most celebrated.
